message:
fix(runtime): harden scheduler and finalizer leak detection

Priority scheduler: replace deterministic first-wins tie-breaking with
randomised selection among equal-priority entries so that co-scheduled
tasks cannot starve peers by always winning insertion-order ties. Adds
a regression test proving the old deterministic policy allowed a single
task to monopolise the lane.

Local queue: fix dead-task leak where a completed task left in the local
queue prevented its slot from being reclaimed, causing the queue to
report incorrect length and skip subsequent steal attempts. Adds targeted
regression test.

Three-lane scheduler: tighten lane-selection logic and clarify priority
escalation comments after audit.

Runtime state: change finalizing-region leak detection to wait until the
owning region's task_count reaches zero rather than merely checking
is_terminal on each task — the previous check fired too early because
task_completed still had to abort orphaned obligations and unlink the
task from the region. Adds a regression test that triggers the premature
leak-detection race.

Kernel/reactor/token: minor formatting and doc-comment clarifications
surfaced during the same audit pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
